column1: How are you? Where are you from?
This is my first time in Nepal.
Is it allowed to take photos here?
My name is Sarah. I came from America.
What is the weather like in Nepal?
I tasted the food. It's good.
Can you take me for a tour?
I want to learn to cook Nepali food.
What kind of things can I find here?
Can you show me the Durbar?
We will stay in Nepal for five days.
I want to learn to trek.
Can you teach me Nepali language?
I want to buy at wholesale price.
Quality is good but price is high.
Can you give it at the mentioned price?
If it's paid in cash, there's some discount.
My customer is looking for good items.
We meet after a long time.
Not bad. There's high demand in the market.
Good, I'll take it. Prepare the documents.
That's not okay. You have to pay cash for me.
Everything is fine, thanks for asking.
Where did you go on vacation?
Okay, I'll show you at home.
There's so much injustice in politics.
Nepal is performing well in sports now.
I also want to watch this movie soon.
My son is good in studies.
My daughter is good at music.
We just bought a new house.
I have some flowers in my hands.
What's the warranty on this item?
How will you pay? Cash or card?
I don't have a card, I'll pay in cash.
Should I pack this in jar or bag?
Here, I've written it on paper.
Excuse me, where is the bus station?
Go straight, it's to the south.
It's to the east from here.
It's on the main road to the north.
Can you show me the right path?
No, it's in the opposite direction.
Try turning here from this junction.
Go straight and turn right at the police station.
Is there a path to the Army from here?

Yes, one son and one daughter.
What do you want to do in future?
I want to be a professor at university.

It would be better if there was more.
Where have you gone on vacation?
I have visited India and Bangladesh.
It was a really good experience.
When will you go there again?
I can't sleep well at night.
